specification.topic-statement: The tool result wrapping system encodes tool execution outcomes in a standard JSON envelope that conveys success or failure.

scope.in-scope: Constructing a success envelope that embeds the original tool output JSON, and constructing a failure envelope that carries an error message and error code.
scope.boundary: This topic constructs JSON documents using the JSON library and serializes them to strings. All allocations use a talloc-backed allocator tied to the caller's context.

field.name: result
field.note: the original tool output JSON, copied verbatim

behavior.wrap-success:
A tool result JSON string is received. It is parsed as a JSON value.
If the tool result string is not valid JSON, the wrapping system produces a failure envelope instead of a success envelope. The failure envelope carries the error message "Tool returned invalid JSON" and the error code "INVALID_OUTPUT".
When JSON parsing fails, the parse result (which is null) is passed to the memory release routine. Releasing a null pointer is a no-op in the memory management system, so no harm results.
A new JSON object is constructed as the root of a new document. A boolean field named "tool_success" is set to true. The parsed tool result value is copied into the new document and added under the key "result". The JSON object is serialized to a string.
The serialized JSON string produced by the JSON library is owned by the library and is copied into the caller's allocation context. The library string and the parsed tool result document are then released. The copied string is returned.
behavior.wrap-failure: An error message string and an error code string are received. A JSON object is constructed as the root of a new document. A boolean field named "tool_success" is set to false. A string field named "error" is set to a copy of the error message. A string field named "error_code" is set to a copy of the error code. The object is serialized to a string, copied into the caller's allocation context, and returned. The library string is released before returning.
